---
title: "About"
---

## Value Statement

Fundamentally, my work is about blending data and egalitarian values to understand public problems, and find solutions conducive to well-being for all. My work is guided by a belief that rigorous, reproducible analysis, made accessible to decision-making stakeholders and the public alike is critical for building a more just and healthy society. Such commitment to policy research and novel knowledge creation is built upon a framework of transparency, rigor, and a will to build a society that maximizes human agency, liberty and well-being.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## About Me

I'm a quantitative policy analyst and researcher focused on the intersection of data, public health and social equity. I have recently completed a Master of Public Policy at the University of Illinois Chicago, specializing in public health policy, where I maintained a GPA of 3.92 and have sharpened skills in the domains of communicationa and analysis. Prior to my graduate studies, I obtained a Bachelor of Arts in Political Science from the University of Illinois Urbana‑Champaign with a concentration in public policy and democratic institutions.

### Professional Experience

I work as a research assistant at the Nathalie P. Voorhees Center for Neighborhood and Community Improvement. There, my main project is ADA‑PARC---a national research effort that examines quality‑of‑life disparities for people with disabilities. My work within this project has involved building a complex U.S. Census data pipeline, a website to display results using R-Shiny, developing interactive visualization dashboards using Tableau, and producing an accessible report using Quarto/LaTex. While at the Voorhees Center I have also contributed to the City of Chicago's Point-In-Time Homeless Count (PIT). Prior to this, I served as a research intern at the Jefferson Educational Society in Erie, Pennsylvania, where I produced publications and assisted with research projects, including an analysis fo the human rights abuses prior to the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar.

### Projects & Interests

My portfolio reflects interests in public health, socioeconomic equity and program evaluation. Recent projects include modeling the correlates of mood disorders and predictors of chronic disease using CDC data, analyzing rent burden across the Chicago metropolitan area, examining support for wealth redistribution using the GSS, and evaluating the potential impact of the Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants and Children (WIC) using synthetic data.

### Skills & Leadership

I bring a combination of policy analysis, quantitative and qualitative research methods, econometrics, and strong written and verbal communication skills. My technical toolkit includes Git, R, Quarto/RStudio, Tableau, Shiny for modeling, visualization and reproducible reporting. Beyond research, I have experience leading and organizing mental health advocacy efforts as president of Active Minds at the University of Illinois Urbana‑Champaign.

Through this portfolio I aim to share insights and tools that contribute to policy solutions conducive to well‑being for all. Feel free to explore my projects and reach out via LinkedIn or email if you would like to connect.
